decision_question: Can an existing Understand-Anything graph be converted into a useful, source-grounded English code walkthrough video of less than three minutes, with less than ten minutes of operator setup after graph generation?
initiating_context: Developers entering an unfamiliar repository must actively inspect many files or manually record explanations; static graphs help exploration but do not create a concise, replayable learning artifact.

target_user.description: Developers, maintainers, technical leads, and AI engineers who need a fast high-level mental model of a codebase.
target_user.usage_context: After an Understand-Anything knowledge graph has been generated and before deep file-by-file exploration.

economic_buyer.description: An engineering or open-source lead accountable for onboarding quality and developer time.
economic_buyer.value_recipient: The developer receives a reusable explanation while the team receives reproducible, source-linked onboarding material.

job_to_be_done: Turn an exact repository snapshot and its knowledge graph into a short narrated walkthrough whose material claims can be traced back to source evidence.
observed_problem: Existing graph exploration still requires active navigation, manual screen recordings become stale, and fluent AI summaries may lack mechanical source grounding or duration guarantees.
current_alternatives: Interactive use of the existing Understand-Anything dashboard
Manually authored documentation or onboarding calls
expected_value: A concise, replayable, provenance-bound learning artifact and a faster path to the first correct architecture discussion; learning improvement remains unproven until observed with users.
strategic_context: The project is a Developer Tools submission that must meaningfully use Codex and GPT-5.6 while clearly separating pre-existing graph analysis from the new video compilation pipeline.

constraints.description: The Build Week submission deadline is 2026-07-21 17:00 PT; the internal build deadline is earlier.
The Hackathon video must be strictly less than 180 seconds and target approximately 165 seconds.
Repository content and graph summaries are untrusted; source access, rendering, and outbound speech must be bounded.
Understand-Anything is pre-existing MIT-licensed work and must be attributed without implying affiliation or trademark rights.
External service spend for the vertical slice may not exceed USD 25; any TTS call requires a narrower declared cap and human authorization.
